title: Heathrow Airport Expansion
tagline: POC microsite for the 110-page June-2019 consultation document. Same content, three home-page approaches: editorial stewardship, IA-led wayfinder, two-track skim/deep-dive.

direction.phrase: Three approaches to a single static home page for the Heathrow Expansion consultation document. Brand-faithful: Frutiger Next type stack, brand-purple #462170, navy chrome, 8px radius, photo-led story tiles.
movements: PDF (110pp) → microsite home (one page; six pillar pages downstream)
type-stack: pinned (Frutiger Next / Open Sans fallback)

originalCritique: The source isn't a tired website — it's a 110-page PDF consultation document from June 2019 (Heathrow's Preferred Masterplan). PDFs do badly online: no in-page wayfinding, no skim path, no estimated reading time, no progressive disclosure. The consultation invited views from communities, passengers, businesses and government — and asked them to read 110 pages to do it. The microsite re-houses the document on the brand's surface and bracket-tests three home-page treatments before the inner pillars are built.

variants.name: Stewardship
variants.thesis: Editorial document-led. Full-bleed sunset hero of the expanded airport, foreword-style intro, six photo-led pillar cards in a 3×2 grid. Closest in feel to heathrow.com/expansion. The brand voice carries the IA — readers arrive into stewardship, not a chooser.
variants.fix: Editorial document-led. Full-bleed sunset hero of the expanded airport, foreword-style intro, six photo-led pillar cards in a 3×2 grid. Closest in feel to heathrow.com/expansion. The brand voice carries the IA — readers arrive into stewardship, not a chooser. Best when the brand statement should land first and the document second.

variants.name: Wayfinder
variants.thesis: IA-led. Two-column hero with a quick-links panel; phase timeline strip; the six pillars rendered as a list with reading-time, sub-section count, and PDF source. For readers who want orientation before they read.
variants.fix: IA-led wayfinder. Two-column hero pairs the brand statement with a quick-links panel; a phase timeline strip lets readers locate themselves in the 2026–2050 programme; the six pillars render as a list with estimated reading time, sub-section count, and source PDF section number. Built for a reader who wants orientation before commitment.

variants.name: Two-Track
variants.thesis: Skim or deep-dive. Hero offers two routes: a five-minute summary or the full proposal. Phase timeline runs through the page. Foreword pull-quote. Built for two reader types in the same surface.
variants.fix: Skim or deep-dive — two reader paths in the same surface. The hero offers two routes immediately: a five-minute summary or the full proposal. The phase timeline runs through the page rather than sitting in a single strip. A foreword pull-quote anchors the brand voice. Best when the consultation has both informed stakeholders and curious neighbours arriving on the same page.

variants.name: Civic Prospectus
variants.thesis: Faithful renewal of the current /expansion section. Full-bleed dark aerial photograph as hero; 14 numbered chapters in a 2-column index, monochromatic and hover-active. Civic-formal voice — British English, declarative, present tense.
variants.fix: Faithful renewal of the current /expansion section. Full-bleed dark aerial photograph as hero with white display headline overlay and primary + ghost CTAs. 14 numbered chapters render in a 2-column monochromatic index with hover-active rows. Civic-formal voice matches the current site register: British English, declarative, present tense. The closest of the four variants in feel to the live heathrow.com/expansion section — the option a brand stakeholder picks to validate that stardust can do faithful before it does inventive.
